irish: Ní neart go cur le chéile
english: There is no strength without unity

irish: Tús maith leath na hoibre
english: A good start is half the work

irish: Giorraíonn beirt bóthar
Níor bhris focal maith fiacail riamh
english: A good word never broke a tooth

irish: Is fearr an tsláinte ná na táinte
Mol an óige agus tiocfaidh sí
english: Praise the young and they will flourish

irish: An té nach bhfuil láidir ní foláir dó bheith glic
english: He who is not strong must be clever

irish: Ar scáth a chéile a mhaireann na daoine
english: People live in each other's shelter

irish: Is minic a bhris béal duine a shrón
english: Many a time a man's mouth broke his nose

irish: Níl aon tinteán mar do thinteán féin
Is fearr Gaeilge briste ná Béarla cliste
english: Broken Irish is better than clever English

irish: Ní hé lá na gaoithe lá na scolb
english: The windy day is not the day for thatching

irish: Is maith an scéalaí an aimsir
An rud is annamh is iontach
Ní bhíonn saoi gan locht
english: There is no wise person without fault

irish: Is fearr cara sa chúirt ná punt sa sparán
english: A friend in court is better than money

irish: Bíonn siúlach scéalach
Is minic a bhíonn an ceart ag an té ciúin
english: The quiet person is often right

irish: Ní bhíonn an rath ach mar a mbíonn an smacht
Is fada an bóthar nach mbíonn casadh ann
Ní bhíonn clú gan chúis
english: There is no fame without reason

irish: An té nach gcuireann san earrach ní bhaineann sé san fhómhar
Is fearr súil romhainn ná dhá shúil inár ndiaidh
Ní bhíonn aon rún nach scaoiltear
Is minic a dhéanann bród duine bocht
english: Pride often makes a person poor

irish: Ní hé an lá báistí lá na bpáistí
english: A rainy day is not a day for children

irish: An té a luíonn le madaí éireoidh sé le dreancaidí
Is fearr mall ná go deo
Is minic a bhíonn an fhírinne searbh
Ní bhíonn aon tinteán mar do thinteán féin
Is maith an t-anlann an t-ocras
Ní bhíonn an rath ach mar a mbíonn an obair
An té a bhíonn siúlach bíonn scéalach
Is fearr greim bia ná dhá ghearrchaint
english: Better a bite to eat than two chats

irish: Ní bhíonn aon phian mar do phian féin
Is minic a bhíonn an t-ádh ar an amadán
An té nach bhfuil ciall aige caithfidh sé fulaingt
Ní thagann ciall roimh aois
english: Wisdom does not come before age

irish: Is fearr an tslí bheag ná an tslí mhór uaireanta
english: Sometimes the small way is better

irish: Is minic a bhíonn bréag ina fírinne
english: A lie can seem like truth

irish: Ní bhíonn aon rud buan ach an t-athrú
Is fearr cairde ná ór
Is minic a thagann an t-ádh gan iarraidh
Ní bhíonn sa saol ach ceo
Is maith an scáthán súil charad
english: A friend's eye is a good mirror

irish: Ní bhíonn grá gan locht
english: There is no love without fault

irish: Is fearr an tsláinte ná na táinte
Is minic a dhíolann duine a anam ar airgead
english: People sell their soul for money

irish: Ní bhíonn aon rud chomh dona nach bhfuil rud éigin maith ann
english: Nothing so bad without some good

irish: Is fearr aithne ná tuairim
Ní bhíonn aon ghaoth nach séideann go maith do dhuine éigin
english: Every wind blows good for someone

irish: Is minic a bhíonn an ceart ag an duine beag
english: The small person is often right

irish: Ní bhíonn aon rud chomh simplí agus a shíltear
english: Nothing is as simple as it seems

irish: Is fearr obair ná caint
Ní bhíonn aon lá gan scéal
Is minic a thagann ciall tar éis na heachtra
Ní bhíonn aon duine gan locht
Is fearr fírinne searbh ná bréag milis
english: Better a bitter truth than a sweet lie

irish: Ní bhíonn aon rud saor ach an bás
